summary: Pre-match setup screen: character grid nav (P1/P2), CPU AI opponent selection, handicap bar (0-400), cheat code engine (31 sequences), practice mode toggle, match start flow.
functions: char_select_init — Main char select screen: load sprites, create cursor/com/confirm objects per game mode. Branching by game_mode (1P, VS, Practice, Team, Tournament). Entry point called from fighters.c.
char_select_cursor — Nav (L/R arrow, A confirm), wraps 0-9, skips locked chars (sonokov/exhibition gates). 1P auto-confirms for CPU.
char_select_cpu_scroll — CPU AI auto-scroll through available chars toward target, random pick when scroll timer expires.
char_select_countdown — 2-digit countdown (tens/ones sprite digits), then triggers char_select_confirm and transitions to match.
char_select_confirm — Store selected char into gWadCondLoad[player].x_eb1fe45b (char ID), increment confirm counter.
char_select_count_avail — Count unlocked chars for player. NON_MATCHING (C decompilation doesn't match binary).
char_select_random — Pick random unlocked char, handles forced picks / duplicate avoids. NON_EQUIVALENT.
handicap_init — Create health bar sprites (part + fill), digit sprites (hundreds/tens/ones), set handicap_input callback.
handicap_input — L/R adjusts health (40-400 range, step 1→28 accelerating), bar sprite position update, SFX on confirm.
handicap_refresh — Calls digit_render, handles A confirm fade-out, updates bar position based on current health.
handicap_digit_render — Health % as 3 sprites (hundreds/tens/ones), leading-zero hiding, panic flash at bounds.
handicap_health_to_width — (health * 80) / 400 → pixel width of health bar fill.
pregame_start_seq — State machine (4 states): idle → model flash → hide → set fight flags + transition screen.
pregame_model_flash — Oscillate frame counter for flash effect before fight start.
practice_transition — Practice flash: fade out when model flash is active, hide sprite.
practice_input — Toggle COM control side (L/R), switch to transition state on confirm.
debug_model_edit — D-pad: move x/y/z. L/R: frame advance. Analog stick tilt: rotation/scale. Debug-only.
cheat_update — Entry point: runs cheat_process for both P1 and P2. Called from menu.c title screen.
cheat_process — Compare input button against sCheatTable, find matching sequence start or advance current.
cheat_advance — Increment step in current cheat sequence, call handler when sequence complete.
cheat_sequence_cmp — Compare two u16 button sequences, return common prefix length.
cheat_set_flags — Handler: set sSonorkUnlocked (char 8) and sDemitronUnlocked (char 1) flags.
cheat_goto_stage — Handler: force char + force CPU opponent, route to stage select screen.
cheat_switch_char — Handler: wrapper calling cheat_goto_stage with target char ID.
cheat_practice_mode — Handler: route to practice screen (char = exhibition placeholder).
cheat_set_difficulty — Handler: set gWadCondLoad difficulty for both players.
cheat_switch_side — Handler: swap P1↔P2 controllers, clear char availability flags, re-route char select.
cheat_navigate — Handler: set opponent char + route to screen ID.

structs.CheatEntry: Cheat table entry: u16* sequence (0xFFFF-terminated button list), handler func ptr, param1 (s16), param2 (s16).
structs.CheatState: Cheat engine state: currentEntry ptr, stepIndex, lastButton (for dedup of held buttons).

globals.sSonorkUnlocked: u8 — char 8 (Sonork) availability gate for char select.
globals.sDemitronUnlocked: u8 — char 1 (Demitron) availability gate for char select.
globals.sPlayerCursorObj[2]: Object* — P1/P2 cursor objects in char select grid.
globals.sLastWinnerIdx: u32 — last round winner index (for rematch char switching).
globals.sCheatTable[30]: CheatEntry[30] — 30 button sequences with handler dispatch. Includes 12 char-switch, 7 difficulty, 10 screen-route, 1 practice, 1 switch-side, 2 char-unlock entries.

subsystems: Character grid: P1/P2 navigation, CPU AI opponent walks grid toward target
Cheat codes: 30 directional input sequences with handler dispatch (char unlock, char switch, difficulty, screen route, practice, side swap)
Game mode: 1P, VS, Practice, Team, Tournament branching
open_questions: debug_model_edit — created/used? May be dead code or created from an external debug menu.
char_select_count_avail — NON_MATCHING: the C decompilation doesn't match the compiled binary's codegen.
char_select_random — NON_EQUIVALENT: the C decompilation may not be functionally identical.
sCheatTable[30] entries still use D_8004Axxx / x_ hashed sequence arrays — rename data array names.
CheatEntry/CheatState struct fields still hash-named — rename when field purposes clearer.
